justification: Stable identity/hashing of a result enables replay and frozen-snapshot comparison; part of existing discipline.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: The strength of claim (observed vs proxy vs candidate) is exactly the honest PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN + proxy-labeling discipline the system already enforces.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Testing capabilities against synthetic scenarios is part of the existing capability-test discipline.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Recording why a result is UNKNOWN is the core honest-UNKNOWN reporting that exists.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Ball height (ground/air) is not modeled; tracking provides planar positions and velocity but no vertical/height state. No ground_air primitive in catalog.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether the ball is moving/stationary and its motion is derivable from ball velocity over tracking.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Ball leaving the field is a region-exit / boundary crossing detectable from ball position vs pitch bounds; an out event also anchors via action_event_anchor.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A ball-contact (touch) event is not a catalog primitive; controlled_pass_episode and one_touch_relay infer completed passes but a generic player-ball contact requires a touch/contact primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A contest between players for the ball is a duel/contest with no primitive; tracking proximity alone cannot establish a contest.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Gaining control corresponds to a possession start, observable via change_across_anchor / possession_segment boundaries; but pinpointing the control-GAIN touch needs a touch/contact primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Losing control is a possession end / turnover, observable via change_across_anchor; but a miscontrol-specific loss (vs a tackled/intercepted loss) needs a touch/contest primitive to disambiguate.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether the ball is dead (out of play / pre-restart) is given by the restart/event anchor and ball motion; action_event_anchor marks dead-ball periods.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A duel is a defensive/physical contest with no primitive (no duel/tackle/interception primitive per guidance).

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A loose/uncontrolled ball state (no team in control) is not a catalog primitive; possession_segment assumes control. Requires a loose-ball primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Distance between a player and the ball is a direct pairwise_distance over tracking.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether a player can reach the ball needs a time-to-arrival/intercept computation; pairwise_distance + velocity give the inputs but the reachability primitive (time_to_arrival) is missing.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A player in control during a pass is captured by controlled_pass_episode legs and one_touch_relay; but a standalone player-control episode (carry-like dwell with the ball) needs a carry/touch primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Instantaneous 'this player has the ball' state is approximated by nearest-player-to-ball within control distance, but firmly assigning control needs a touch/contact primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Change of possession is directly the change_across_anchor over possession_segment boundaries (turnover/regain).

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Continuity/retention over a window is composed from possession_segment + action_chain (same-possession, never bridges turnover) + change_across_anchor aggregated.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: An ordered sequence of actions within a possession is exactly action_chain / pass_chain_episode (same-possession ordered).

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Comparing possession labels across providers is a provenance/conflict governance artifact; the evidence/conflict-record discipline exists.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Regaining control after a loss is a possession-gain anchor (change_across_anchor), but distinguishing an active recovery (winning a loose ball / interception) from a clean restart needs a loose-ball/interception primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether two events are in the same possession is directly enforced by possession_segment / action_chain (never bridges turnover).

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A second-ball situation is a loose-ball contest after a first contact; needs loose_ball + contest primitives that do not exist.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Which team is in control at a time is the possession_segment team label.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A contiguous team-possession episode is directly possession_segment.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A generic touch event is not a primitive; one_touch_relay and controlled_pass infer pass-related touches, but a standalone touch detection needs a touch/contact primitive.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: velocity exists (displacement-derived) but true second-derivative acceleration is not in catalog; per calibration acceleration specifically = missing_primitive.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Episode of sustained acceleration requires the acceleration primitive (second derivative) which does not exist; persists_for over velocity gives speed-magnitude windows only, not acceleration.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Change-of-direction requires heading/turn-rate derivation beyond velocity magnitude; per calibration change-of-direction leans missing_primitive (acceleration/heading derivative).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Closing speed is delivered by pressure_on_carrier (nearest-defender closing_speed) and by relative velocity via pairwise_distance slope + velocity; kinematics family strength.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Convergence of two entities = pairwise_distance decreasing over time (negative slope), readable via pairwise_distance + velocity with lte/gt operators.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Divergence = pairwise_distance increasing over time; same chain as convergence with opposite sign, using pairwise_distance + velocity.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Heading is the direction component of the displacement-derived velocity vector; velocity primitive already carries directional velocity, not just speed.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Speed-threshold run sustained over time = velocity magnitude gte threshold persists_for; pure velocity-magnitude band, a kinematics strength.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Jerk is the third derivative of position; velocity is first derivative only, acceleration itself missing, so jerk is doubly out of reach.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Lateral component of displacement-derived velocity vector; velocity primitive supplies the vector, lateral axis is the pitch-width component (cf. signed_lateral_shift framing).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Longitudinal component of the displacement-derived velocity vector; velocity primitive supplies it, axis is pitch-length direction.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Onset = velocity magnitude rising across a low threshold (start of motion); detectable from velocity + gt rising-edge, no acceleration value required.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Stop = velocity magnitude falling below a near-zero threshold and persisting; velocity + lte + persists_for, no deceleration derivative needed.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Velocity vectors of multiple players are available so co-directed/co-timed motion is partially expressible, but a declared synchrony measure (correlated velocity across an entity set) is not a primitive and needs a composition rule over per-entity velocity.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Net displacement = vector between start and end position over a window; velocity is displacement-derived so the underlying displacement over lookback is directly available.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Integrated travelled distance along the path needs a trajectory/arc-length primitive; velocity gives per-step displacement but accumulating arc length over a window is not a declared operator (integral is in temporal-logic family but trajectory accumulation is not wired here).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Raw tracking position is the base modality (tracking positions AVAILABLE); all geometric primitives consume it directly.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Predicting future trajectory is a learned/probabilistic model; learned models are explicitly NOT an available modality.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Relative velocity between two entities = difference of their velocity vectors, or pairwise_distance slope; velocity + pairwise_distance deliver it (basis of closing_speed).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Rate of growing separation = positive component of pairwise_distance slope (relative velocity); inverse of closing_speed, same supported chain.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Speed = magnitude of displacement-derived velocity; explicit catalog strength per calibration (velocity primitive, UNKNOWN when sparse).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Sprint = velocity magnitude above a high threshold sustained; velocity + gte + persists_for. (Onset-acceleration criterion optional; magnitude-band definition is supported.)

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Change_point operator exists in temporal logic and velocity-direction is available, but a declared change-point over the velocity-heading signal (turn detection) is not wired; needs composition rule binding change_point to heading-of-motion.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Curvature is a geometric second-order property of the path (turn rate over speed); requires acceleration/turn-rate primitive not present.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Comparing path shapes requires a trajectory/path primitive and a similarity measure over it; no carry/trajectory primitive in catalog.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Turn rate is the time-derivative of heading (angular acceleration of motion direction); a second-order kinematic, in the acceleration family, not derivable from first-derivative velocity alone.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Direct catalog primitive: velocity (displacement-derived speed/velocity over lookback; UNKNOWN sparse).

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Deceleration is a negative-acceleration episode; per calibration deceleration leans missing_primitive (needs acceleration), not derivable from velocity magnitude alone as a signed second derivative.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Direct equivalent of action_event_anchor; anchors an action to a time/location from events+tracking.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Ball path between action origin and destination is reconstructable from ball tracking positions anchored to the action; ball_lateral_fraction + tracking + action_event_anchor cover the ball leg (cf. controlled_pass_episode ball leg).

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Pass/touch/relay candidates are supported via controlled_pass_episode/one_touch_relay_episode/action_event_anchor, but a generic action candidate also spans duels/tackles/shots/carries which have no primitive; partial coverage by action family.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Destination of an action = relation_destination_entry + action_event_anchor end location; passes/touches already reconstruct destinations.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Duration between action onset and end anchors = persists_for over action_event_anchor span; available for the supported action types.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: A taxonomy/hierarchy over action types (pass vs duel vs shot vs carry) presumes those action primitives exist and a declared classification scheme; duel/tackle/shot/carry primitives are missing so the hierarchy cannot be populated.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Ground vs air can be proxied from ball vertical motion, but ball height is not a catalog primitive (ball_lateral_fraction is side/width only); needs a ball-height signal to be reliable.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: A set of competing action hypotheses is essentially a probabilistic/learned reconstruction over multiple missing action types; no learned model and missing duel/tackle/shot/carry primitives.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Origin location/actor of an action = action_event_anchor start; supported for passes/touches/relays.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Outcome of an action (completed/lost) = outcome_classification anchored to the action; available for pass/touch actions.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Change of controlling team can be inferred from possession_segment + outcome_classification (turnover), but distinguishing a contested recovery from a clean pass interception needs a duel/contest primitive; coarse recovery partial, contested recovery missing.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: A block is a contact/contest action on the ball; no contact/duel primitive to reconstruct it (only ball-trajectory deflection inference, which is unmodeled).

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Carry (a player advancing in control of the ball) is an explicitly named missing primitive (carry_episode); no carry primitive in catalog.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Clearance reconstruction is explicitly called out as missing_primitive (no clearance primitive); requires a defensive-action primitive.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Controlled action (player in control performing an action) maps to controlled_pass_episode / one_touch_relay_episode family for the supported action types.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: A cross is a pass type from wide areas; the pass leg is supported (controlled_pass_episode + ball_lateral_fraction wide origin), but identifying it as an aerial cross needs ball-height and a declared cross definition; ground cross partial, aerial cross gap.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Cutback is a pass from byline back into a central area; pass leg supported via controlled_pass_episode + ball_lateral_fraction + geometric_progressive_corridor, but the named cutback geometry/region is a dynamic region (cutback_region) which is missing; pass partial, region gap.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Deflection requires detecting a contact altering ball trajectory; no contact primitive and no probabilistic trajectory-break model.
closest_supported_substitute: ball tracking (trajectory only, no contact attribution)

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Dribble-attempt reconstruction is explicitly missing_primitive (no dribble-attempt primitive); requires a carry/take-on action primitive.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Duel reconstruction is explicitly missing_primitive (no duel primitive); contest between two players for the ball not modeled.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Identifying a header needs ball-height plus contact at a player's head; ball-height absent and contact/body modality absent (no biomechanics/body model).

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: An interception = opponent enters the pass lane and takes control mid-pass; pass leg + change of control are supported (controlled_pass_episode + outcome_classification + change_across_anchor), but attributing the interception contact needs a contact/contest primitive; coarse interception partial.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Loss of control on receipt can be proxied by a reception followed by immediate loss (outcome_classification + possession_segment break), but confirming it was a touch error vs a tackle needs a contact/touch-error model; proxy partial.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Direct equivalent of controlled_pass_episode; pass reconstruction is an explicit catalog strength.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Reception = receiver gaining control at pass end; supported via controlled_pass_episode end + relation_destination_entry + possession_segment.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Release = ball leaving the carrier at pass/relay onset; supported via controlled_pass_episode onset + one_touch_relay_episode + action_event_anchor.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Rebound requires detecting ball coming off a player/keeper/post (a contact/deflection event); no contact primitive.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Shot reconstruction is explicitly missing_primitive (no shot action primitive / shot_geometry); a shot-action primitive is required.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Switch as an action maps to switch_of_play + controlled_pass_episode (the long cross-pitch pass leg); both in catalog.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Tackle reconstruction is explicitly missing_primitive (no tackle primitive); a defensive contest/contact action.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: A through ball is a penetrating pass into space behind a line; supported via controlled_pass_episode + geometric_progressive_corridor + relative_position_to_line + controlled_line_break_episode.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Absolute pitch coordinates are the base tracking frame (tracking positions AVAILABLE); the raw coordinate frame is directly available.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Actor-relative frame is a general reference-frame transform, explicitly called out as missing (reference_frame); only ball-relative side and team-centroid frames exist.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: A team-centroid frame exists for the defensive team (defensive_outfield_centroid); an attacking-team frame is the same construction applied to the other team plus attacking-direction normalization, which is a typed reference_frame gap (general team/attacking-direction frame).

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Ball-relative side/width is supported (ball_lateral_fraction), but a full ball-relative coordinate frame (transforming all positions into ball-centered axes) is the general reference_frame primitive that is missing; simple side supported, full transform gap.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Ball-side region (which side of the pitch the ball is on) is the simple side/width case = ball_lateral_fraction; per calibration simple side/width = supported.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Behind-unit region is explicitly a named dynamic region (behind-unit region) called out as missing_primitive (structured_zones); only line-relative position exists, not a region object.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Between-lines/between-units pocket is explicitly a named dynamic region called out as missing_primitive (structured_zones); multi_line_model gives lines but not the pocket region between them.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: A general user-defined dynamic region is precisely the structured_zones primitive that is missing; no declarable dynamic-region constructor in catalog.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: The cutback zone (pocket near the byline/edge of box from which cutbacks are played) is a named dynamic football region; structured_zones primitive missing. Fixed-coordinate approximation possible but the canonical dynamic cutback region is not a catalog primitive.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Defending-team frame is exactly defensive_outfield_centroid (team-centroid frame for the defending team), a catalog primitive providing a team reference frame.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: A corridor toward goal partly overlaps geometric_progressive_corridor (a supported fixed corridor), but a named goal-mouth/shot corridor as a region with goal anchoring is a structured_zone; corridor primitive partial, named goal region gap.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Goal-relative coordinate frame (angle/distance-to-goal axes) is a general reference_frame transform not in catalog; no goal-anchored frame primitive.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Outside-the-block region is a named dynamic region derived from team shape; structured_zones primitive missing. team_compactness gives a scalar, not a region with inside/outside membership.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Penalty area is a static, pitch-fixed zone defined by laws-of-the-game coordinates; a fixed rectangle on the absolute pitch frame, expressible as a constant region with membership tests (no dynamic primitive needed).

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Pitch thirds are static pitch-fixed bands (defensive/middle/attacking) on absolute coordinates; fixed-coordinate membership, no dynamic-region primitive required.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Arrival into a region maps to relation_destination_entry/change_across_anchor for supported (static or corridor) regions, but for named dynamic regions it inherits the structured_zones gap; supported for fixed zones, partial for dynamic ones.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Dwell = membership persisting over time (persists_for over region membership); supported for static/corridor regions but inherits the structured_zones gap for named dynamic regions.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Entry into a region = relation_destination_entry / change_across_anchor for fixed and corridor regions (supported), but dynamic-region entry inherits structured_zones gap.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Exit = membership falling edge (change_across_anchor) for fixed/corridor regions (supported); dynamic-region exit inherits structured_zones gap.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Membership test is supported for static zones and attacking lanes (lane_occupancy) and corridors, but a general region-membership operator over arbitrary dynamic regions needs structured_zones; supported for fixed, partial for dynamic.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Vacating a region = last occupant leaving (membership count -> 0, change_across_anchor) for fixed/corridor regions (supported); dynamic-region case inherits structured_zones gap.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Rest-defense region is a named dynamic region (the area occupied by defenders behind the ball during attack); a structured_zone derived from team shape and ball position, not a catalog primitive.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Static pitch zone is a fixed-coordinate region on the absolute pitch frame; constant geometry with membership tests, no dynamic primitive needed.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Interior of the team shape (inside the block) is a dynamic region requiring a shape/hull region object; team_compactness yields a scalar, not an inside/outside region. structured_zones missing.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: A frame relative to a defensive unit/line is a general reference_frame transform; multi_line_model provides lines and relative_position_to_line a scalar, but a full unit-anchored coordinate frame is not a catalog primitive.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Vertical channels (left/center/right longitudinal bands) are close to attacking lanes which lane_occupancy supports; as static longitudinal bands they are supported, but if defined as half-space dynamic channels they need structured_zones. Treated as fixed bands = mostly supported with a typed gap for half-space variants.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Weak side (far side from the ball) is a ball-relative dynamic region; ball_lateral_fraction gives ball side as a scalar but the far-side region as a structured dynamic zone (with extent/membership) is structured_zones, missing.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Zone 14 is a fixed pitch-coordinate region (central area just outside the box); static rectangle on the absolute pitch frame with membership tests, no dynamic primitive needed.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Formation typing (assigning a labeled shape such as 4-3-3 / probability distribution over formations) is not derivable from compactness, ranked lines, or centroid; it requires a formation template/classification primitive.
closest_supported_substitute: multi_line_model + team_compactness give line counts and width/depth but no formation label

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Detecting a change from one labeled formation to another presupposes formation typing, which is absent. Even with change_across_anchor you can flag structural change but not name it as a formation transition.
closest_supported_substitute: change_across_anchor over multi_line_model/team_compactness flags structural change without formation labels

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Directly the width dispersion measure of team_compactness (width/depth/dispersion).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Exact catalog primitive lane_occupancy (attacking lanes/channels).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: multi_line_model exposes ranked lines; rank-1 deepest defensive line plus defensive_line_model (single observed line) gives the last line directly.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: A line dropping (height decreasing over time) is change_across_anchor / signed_lateral_shift-style delta on the multi_line_model line height; longitudinal line-height change is observable.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Whether a line stays straight/intact is partially readable from multi_line_model line fit residual, but a declared straightness/integrity metric beyond rank membership is not a catalog primitive.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Lateral shift of a line is exactly signed_lateral_shift (block shift) applied to a multi_line_model line.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Staggering (how non-flat a line is, per-player offset pattern) needs an explicit staggering/integrity metric; ranked-line membership alone does not quantify stagger. Calibration: staggering = missing_primitive.
closest_supported_substitute: relative_position_to_line per player on a multi_line_model line

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: A line stepping up (height increasing) is the dual of line_drop: change_across_anchor on multi_line_model line height.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: A grid/matrix of zone occupation requires structured pitch zones; catalog has attacking lane_occupancy but no general zone grid. Calibration: occupation of zones = missing_primitive (structured_zones).
closest_supported_substitute: lane_occupancy covers attacking channels only, not a full zone grid

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: The offside line is the second-rear-most defender line; multi_line_model (rank1/2) plus relative_position_to_line gives the last-defender line and beyond-line relations.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Signed longitudinal distance of an attacker relative to the last defensive line = relative_position_to_line against the multi_line_model last line.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Counting players ahead of the ball is relative_position_to_line vs the ball's lateral/longitudinal frame with count_at_least; ball-relative ahead/behind is observable from positions.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Dual of players_ahead_of_ball; behind-ball relation + count. support_arrival_relation already encodes BEHIND_BALL_OUTLET directionality.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Geometry of the rearmost non-pressing players is computable as team_compactness/centroid on that subset, but identifying WHICH players constitute rest-defense needs a role/reference selection that is not a catalog primitive.
closest_supported_substitute: team_compactness + defensive_outfield_centroid on a player subset

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Compactness decreasing (area/width/depth shrinking) over an anchor = change_across_anchor on team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Generic non-rigid deformation (vs translation/scale) is partially captured by change in team_compactness dispersion, but a declared deformation metric (shape (dis)similarity beyond width/depth) is not in the catalog.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Dual of contraction: compactness increasing = change_across_anchor on team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Bulk movement of the block is signed_lateral_shift (block shift) plus centroid displacement = change_across_anchor on defensive_outfield_centroid.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Block area is a compactness/dispersion measure exposed by team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Directly defensive_outfield_centroid (and the centroid notion generally).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Depth/length of the block is the depth component of team_compactness (width/depth/dispersion).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Ratio of spread along principal axes is partially inferable from width vs depth in team_compactness, but a true anisotropy/covariance-ellipse eigen-ratio is not an explicit primitive.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Orientation of the principal axis of the block needs a covariance/ellipse primitive; width/depth give axis lengths but not a declared orientation angle.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Comparing a current shape to a template/another instant requires a shape-matching/similarity primitive not present; width/depth scalars cannot establish configurational similarity.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Skew (asymmetry to one side) is partially captured by signed_lateral_shift of the centroid vs geometric center, but a declared distributional skew metric of the shape is not a primitive.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Stretch (mean distance to centroid / dispersion) is the dispersion output of team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Directly the width component of team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Curvature of a unit's line is a shape/integrity descriptor beyond ranked-line membership; needs a line-integrity/curvature metric (same family as staggering).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Detecting horizontal units/lines is exactly what multi_line_model does (ranked lines, with honest rank-N not observed).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Longitudinal height of a detected unit/line is a direct attribute of multi_line_model ranked lines.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Assigning players to ranked lines is the membership output of multi_line_model.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Equivalent to multi_line_model (ranked lines rank1,2,...).

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Orientation/tilt of a unit needs the line-integrity/orientation metric; ranked-line membership alone gives membership and height, not the line's angle.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Inter-line spacing between SPECIFIC ranked lines is computable as the height difference between multi_line_model lines. Calibration: inter-line spacing via multi_line_model lines = supported.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Lateral span of a unit's members is team_compactness (width) computed on the multi_line_model line membership subset.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Longitudinal compactness = depth component of team_compactness.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Coverage/numbers on the far side from the ball is computable via ball_lateral_fraction defining weak side + local_number_relation/count there, but 'balance' as a declared metric and the weak-side region reference need explicit definition.
required_missing_capability: weak_side region reference + balance metric definition

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Ball-relative ahead/behind is a positional relation from tracking positions; relative_position_to_line in the ball frame and support_arrival_relation already encode it.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Pure geometry from positions and goal location; derivable from pairwise_distance/relative geometry. Goal is a fixed reference, no role taxonomy needed.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Directly ball_lateral_fraction (ball side/width).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Direction from one point to another is basic geometry derivable from positions/velocity; trivially composed from tracking positions.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Dual of ahead_of_ball; ball-relative behind relation, also embedded in support_arrival_relation (BEHIND_BALL_OUTLET).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Whether an entity lies between two others is a positional/geometric relation from pairwise_distance and ordering along an axis.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Clearance of a capsule (e.g., a pass corridor) from defenders is geometric distance between a swept region and points; geometric_progressive_corridor + pairwise_distance cover corridor clearance.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Geometric centroid of a point set; defensive_outfield_centroid is the catalog instance and centroid generalizes from positions.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: A concave (alpha) hull is a specific geometric construction not in the catalog; team_compactness gives dispersion/area but not the hull polygon. Convex/area proxies exist but concave boundary is an added geometry primitive.
closest_supported_substitute: team_compactness (dispersion/area) as a coarse extent proxy

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Convex hull polygon/area is standard geometry from positions and is closely proxied by team_compactness area, but an explicit hull-polygon primitive (for inside/outside tests) is not declared.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Second-moment ellipse (orientation + anisotropy of a point cloud) is not a catalog primitive; team_compactness gives scalar width/depth, not eigenstructure.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Delaunay triangulation adjacency is a specific computational-geometry structure absent from the catalog; pairwise_distance gives distances but not the triangulation graph.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Directly pairwise_distance.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: pairwise_distance to a fixed goal reference point.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Distance to nearest opponent (radius of clear space) is pairwise_distance / nearest-entity; a purely positional openness measure, not a control field.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Unobstructed goal-mouth angle is geometric (angle subtended minus blockers), computable from positions and goal posts, but needs a declared line-of-sight/occlusion blocking model rather than mere distance.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Whether a defender is on the goal side of an attacker is relative_position_to_line in the goal frame (longitudinal ordering toward own goal).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Inside/outside the team shape needs a shape boundary (hull) to test against; team_compactness gives extent scalars but not a polygon for membership tests.
required_missing_capability: convex/concave hull polygon for membership test

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: k-NN by distance is ranking on pairwise_distance with top_k/rank operators.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Geometric line-of-sight (no body between two points) is computable from positions as a corridor-clearance test, but a declared occlusion/segment-blocking primitive is needed; not gaze (which would be modality).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Count of entities within a radius is pairwise_distance + count_at_least; local crowding from positions.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: [audit-corrected] argmin of pairwise_distance. — relies on an aggregation/extremum operator absent from the catalog (only gt/gte/lte/eq/neq/persists_for/exists/count_at_least exist).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Area of overlap between two regions/shapes requires polygon-area geometry (hulls or control regions); catalog has no general area-intersection primitive.
required_missing_capability: polygon area / intersection geometry (hull or control region)

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Adjacency between defined regions presupposes structured zones/regions; only attacking lane_occupancy exists, not a general region topology.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Crossing INTO/through a region is supported by relation_destination_entry (ball-enters-region-in-window) and geometric_progressive_corridor for defined corridors, but arbitrary structured-zone crossings need structured_zones.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Distance to a region boundary is geometric, but requires region definitions; for catalog corridors/anchors it is supported, for arbitrary zones it needs structured_zones.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Bearing of one entity relative to another's frame is geometry from positions and velocity heading (no body orientation needed).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Minimum distance from defenders to a line segment (e.g., pass line) = geometric_progressive_corridor clearance / pairwise_distance to a segment.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Signed lateral distance between entities is the lateral component of pairwise_distance / ball_lateral_fraction-style signed width; signed_lateral_shift demonstrates signed lateral measurement exists.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Signed forward/back distance = relative_position_to_line longitudinal component / pairwise_distance projected on the goal axis.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Entropy of a spatial distribution is a derived dispersion statistic; team_compactness dispersion is a proxy, but a declared entropy primitive (requires a binning/zone basis) is not present.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Ordering entities along an axis is rank on a positional projection (relative_position_to_line) — exactly how multi_line_model ranks lines.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Voronoi/pitch-control partitioning is a computational-geometry construct (and approaches control-field territory) not in the catalog; nearest-entity gives the seed assignment but not the cell geometry.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Centroid with weights is a generalization of defensive_outfield_centroid; mean over weighted positions composes from existing aggregation.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Ball passing beyond a ranked line is multi_line_model + relative_position_to_line change across the leg; controlled_line_break_episode / receiver_line_transition_during_pass_leg cover ball-crossing-line.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Directly controlled_line_break_episode.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Dynamics of a named gap narrowing requires explicit gap-width measurement over time; catalog has no gap_model. Calibration: named gap dynamics = missing_primitive (gap_model).

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Margin by which a defender can close a gap mixes gap geometry with reachability; needs gap_model and/or time_to_arrival.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Longitudinal extent between lines is the inter-line spacing readable from multi_line_model line heights; the depth of a between-lines pocket is partial via lines, but a declared gap depth metric is the gap_model.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Exploiting a gap requires identifying and measuring the named gap then a player/ball entering it; needs gap_model. Calibration: gap opening/exploitation = missing_primitive.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: A gap opening = gap-width increasing over time; requires gap_model explicit width measurement.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Explicit lateral gap width between adjacent unit members is the core gap measurement absent from the catalog. Calibration: gap WIDTH = missing_primitive (gap_model).
closest_supported_substitute: pairwise_distance between adjacent same-line defenders (raw proxy, no gap semantics)

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Spacing between two specific ranked lines is the inter-line height delta from multi_line_model (supported as spacing), but as a navigable 'gap' with width/depth semantics it needs gap_model. Calibration: between-lines via multi_line lines = partial.
required_missing_capability: gap_model (for gap semantics beyond line spacing)

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Gap between two adjacent players within the same line is exactly the lateral gap-width measurement that requires gap_model; pairwise_distance gives raw spacing without gap identification.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Classifying HOW a line was broken (pass vs carry vs dribble through gap) is partially supported: controlled_line_break_episode plus action_event_anchor/action_chain distinguishes pass vs carry, but a 'through-gap vs around' mode needs gap_model.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: State of the receiver beyond a broken line is receiver_line_transition_during_pass_leg + relative_position_to_line + multi_line_model; calibration: receiver-beyond-2nd-line = supported.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Fixing/pinning a line (preventing it stepping/shifting) is partly observable as absence of line_shift/line_step under an attacking presence, but attributing the 'fixing' intent/cause needs a role/reference or causal model.
closest_supported_substitute: multi_line_model line-height stability + relative_position_to_line of the fixer

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Line re-forming after a break is change_across_anchor on multi_line_model line integrity/height following a controlled_line_break_episode anchor.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Counting how many ranked lines an action passes is multi_line_model + relative_position_to_line before/after + count; opponents_bypassed_by_action aligns.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: How far beyond a line the ball/receiver advances is relative_position_to_line longitudinal margin against the multi_line_model last line.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Keeping possession after penetrating a line is action_chain (same-possession) continuing past a controlled_line_break_episode with outcome_classification; does not bridge turnover (consistent with action_chain).

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Support arriving behind the ball after penetration is support_arrival_relation (BEHIND_BALL_OUTLET) sequenced after the break anchor.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: A player moving beyond a ranked line is relative_position_to_line transition vs multi_line_model; analogous to receiver_line_transition and run_beyond_last_line.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Receiving beyond a line is receiver_line_transition_during_pass_leg + relative_position_to_line; calibration: receiver-beyond-2nd-line = supported.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Exact catalog primitive relative_position_to_line.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Position relative to a ranked unit/line is relative_position_to_line against the multi_line_model unit (a unit being a line).

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Identifying WHICH ranked line was broken uses multi_line_model rank labels (rank1,2,…) with controlled_line_break_episode; honest 'rank-N not observed' handles missing lines.

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: A unit pulled out of position is partially captured by line_shift/line_step deltas and line-integrity loss, but a declared dislocation metric (how far a unit deviates from its expected line) needs the line-integrity/gap metric.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Attacking set-piece phase is anchored on a restart/dead-ball event; action_event_anchor on a set-piece event plus possession_segment gives in-possession set-piece windows from events.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Build-up = in-possession in own/defensive third; possession_segment gives in-possession but third-of-pitch zoning needs structured_zones. Calibration: build-up phase partially via zones = partial (structured_zones).

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Counterattack phase is a transition state triggered by a regain; requires a transition_anchor to mark possession change and the fast-progression window. Calibration: counterattack phase = missing_primitive (transition_anchor).
closest_supported_substitute: possession_segment boundary + switch_of_play/velocity (progression proxy, no transition semantics)

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Counterpress = pressing immediately after losing the ball; needs a transition_anchor (loss moment) to scope the press window. pressure_on_carrier exists but the post-loss framing requires the transition anchor.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Recovering shape after losing the ball is a transition/reorganization state requiring a transition_anchor at the loss plus a shape-reformation window.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Defending a set piece is anchored on the restart event (action_event_anchor) while out of possession (possession_segment).

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Final-third phase = in possession with ball in attacking third; possession_segment plus a thirds zoning, but the third boundary needs structured_zones. Calibration: final-third phase partial.
closest_supported_substitute: possession_segment + relation_destination_entry into a final-third region (if defined)

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Time-distribution over goalkeeper-specific phases requires identifying the goalkeeper, which needs a player ROLE taxonomy that is explicitly unavailable.
closest_supported_substitute: possession_segment + positions (cannot single out the GK)

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Block-height phases (high block) are derivable from multi_line_model last-line height / defensive_outfield_centroid being advanced while out of possession; the height thresholding is a block-height phase typing the calibration flags as missing/partial. Lines give the height directly, so partial with thresholding definition needed.
required_missing_capability: block-height phase typing (thresholds on line height)

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: High press = out of possession pressing high up the pitch; pressure_on_carrier + multi_line_model line height + possession_segment give the ingredients, but a press-height phase typing (threshold on where pressure occurs) needs definition.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Contested/loose-ball phase distribution needs a contest state; possession_segment distinguishes in/out of possession but a neutral 'contested' state is not an explicit primitive, requiring a contest definition.
closest_supported_substitute: possession_segment (binary in/out, no contested state) + pressure_on_carrier

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Fraction of time in possession is directly possession_segment aggregated. Calibration: possession on/off = supported.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Low block = out of possession with a deep last line; multi_line_model last-line height + defensive_outfield_centroid deep + possession_segment give it, pending a block-height threshold typing.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Low press = engaging the ball only in deeper areas; pressure_on_carrier location + multi_line_model height + possession_segment, pending press-height threshold typing.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Mid block = intermediate last-line height out of possession; same multi_line_model height basis, needs block-height threshold typing.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Mid press = pressing engagement at midfield height; pressure_on_carrier + line height + possession_segment, pending press-height threshold typing.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Whether the defensive block is set/organized vs scrambling is partly inferable from line_integrity/compactness stability, but 'organized' as a declared state needs a structure-integrity/settling metric (line integrity is itself a typed gap).

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Complement of in-possession; possession_segment aggregated. Calibration: possession on/off = supported.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Projecting evidence onto phases is a meta/governance construct whose meaning depends on the phase set and projection semantics; not a concrete football measurement until defined.
required_missing_capability: explicit definition of phase set + projection semantics

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Possession-based phase transitions (in<->out) are supported via possession_segment boundaries, but transition-state phases (counter/counterpress) and block-height phases need transition_anchor / structured_zones, so transitions among the full phase set are only partial.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Progression phase = in possession advancing through middle third; possession_segment + forward velocity/switch_of_play give advancement, but the zoned middle-third framing needs structured_zones. Calibration: progression phase partial (structured_zones).

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: An embedding of team behavioral state is a learned/probabilistic model output, which is explicitly an unavailable modality.
closest_supported_substitute: team_compactness + multi_line_model + possession_segment as raw features (not an embedding)
